user_query:
I'm a marketing student coordinating a group project. Please help me improve our team collaboration by producing three artifacts from the files in input/: (1) concise meeting minutes and (2) a structured action item list from our latest transcript, and (3) a prioritized task list to guide next-week follow‑ups.

Inputs:
- input/meeting_transcript.md: the raw notes from our 2026-05-01 sync, including participants, DECISION and ACTION lines.
- input/tasks.csv: our current task list. The dependencies column is a '|' separated list of task IDs (empty if none).
- input/config.yaml: scoring rules and meeting tag prefixes. Use config.yaml's values exactly; do not use the system date.

Your outputs (create them under out/):
1) out/meeting_minutes.md
   - Include sections: Participants, Decisions, Action Items.
   - Extract Participants from the line prefixed by meeting.participants_prefix in config.yaml.
   - Decisions: list each line starting with meeting.decision_prefix, verbatim after the prefix.
   - Action Items: list each line starting with meeting.action_prefix with fields: task_id (the #T-XXX tag if present), assignee, due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), and a brief description trimmed of the trailing 'by <date>' phrase.
2) out/action_items.csv
   - Columns in this order: task_id,assignee,due_date,description
   - Each row corresponds to one ACTION extracted as above (use the same parsing as in the minutes). If a field is missing, leave it blank.
3) out/prioritized_tasks.csv
   - Filter tasks to only those whose status is in config.yaml's open_statuses.
   - Compute the following derived fields using config.yaml:
     • today = config.today (use this as the reference date; do not use your system date)
     • days_until_due = (due_date - today) in whole days (overdue if negative)
     • unresolved_dep_count = count of dependency IDs in tasks.csv whose status is not 'Done' (treat missing IDs as unresolved); dependencies is '|' separated.
     • overdue = true if days_until_due < 0, else false
     • due_urgency: if days_until_due >= 0 then max(0, config.due_window_days - days_until_due) else config.due_window_days
     • score = (weights.priority_weight * priority) + (weights.due_soon_weight * due_urgency) + (weights.unresolved_deps_weight * unresolved_dep_count) + (weights.blocked_penalty if status == 'Blocked' else 0) + (weights.overdue_bonus if overdue else 0)
   - Include columns in this exact order: id,title,owner,due_date,status,priority,days_until_due,unresolved_dep_count,overdue,score
   - Sort by score descending and write the top 10 rows (or all if fewer).

Assumptions and constraints:
- All configurable values (today, open_statuses, weights, due_window_days, and meeting tag prefixes) must be read from input/config.yaml. Do not hardcode them.
- Parse task IDs in action lines using the literal pattern '#T-XXX' where XXX are digits; if not present, task_id is blank.
- Treat dependencies as '|' separated IDs in tasks.csv; empty means no dependencies.
- Do not fetch anything from the Internet.

Deliverables recap:
- out/meeting_minutes.md (participants, decisions, action items)
- out/action_items.csv (parsed ACTION lines)
- out/prioritized_tasks.csv (filtered, scored, and ranked tasks per config)

This should give me clear meeting notes and a ranked backlog we can act on next week. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
